revision.text: #REDIRECT [[厳島]]
#[[広島県]][[佐伯郡]][[宮島町]]。「安芸の宮島」として知られる[[日本三景]]の一つ。[[瀬戸内海]]広島湾南西部の[[島]]、[[厳島]]の通称。 #日本人の[[姓]]の一つ

revision.comment: #REDIRECT [[厳島]]
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[厳島]]

'''宮島'''（みやじま、みやしま）
*日本人の[[姓]]の一つ *日本の地名の一つ **[[新潟県]][[上越市]][[板倉区]]宮島 **[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]宮島 *[[厳島]]の別名

revision.comment: +{{aimai}}

revision.text:
'''宮島'''（みやじま、みやしま）。 古くは「宮嶋」とも表記し、[[名字]]としてはこちらを用いることもある。
== 島 == * 宮島 - [[広島県]]の[[島]]、「[[厳島]]」の一般的な呼び名。[[安芸の宮島]]。
== 地名 == * 宮島 (上越市) - [[新潟県]][[上越市]][[板倉区]]の地名。 * 宮島 (豊岡市) - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名。
== 姓、名字 == *[[宮島竜治]] - 編集技師、[[映画]][[監督]] *[[宮島喬]] - [[社会学]]者 *[[宮島里奈]] - 女性[[お笑い芸人]]
== 列車 == *急行「宮島」 - 旧[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]の[[急行列車]]（[[1972年]]廃止）。[[山陽本線優等列車沿革]]を参照。
== 駅名 == *[[宮島駅]] - [[JR西日本宮島フェリー]][[宮島航路]]の駅 *[[宮島駅_(長野県)]] - [[上田温泉電気軌道青木線]]の駅（[[1938年]]廃止） *宮島駅 - 現在の[[宮島口駅]]の、[[1942年]]までの名称
{{aimai}}

'''宮島'''（みやじま、みやしま）。 古くは「宮嶋」とも表記し、[[名字]]としてはこちらを用いることもある。
== 島 == * 宮島 - [[広島県]]の[[島]]、「[[厳島]]」の一般的な呼び名。[[安芸の宮島]]。
== 地名 == * 宮島 (上越市) - [[新潟県]][[上越市]][[板倉区]]の地名。 * 宮島 (豊岡市) - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名。
== 姓、名字 == *[[宮島竜治]] - 編集技師、[[映画]][[監督]] *[[宮島喬]] - [[社会学]]者 *[[宮島里奈]] - 女性[[お笑い芸人]]
== 列車 == *急行「宮島」 - 旧[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]の[[急行列車]]（[[1972年]]廃止）。[[山陽本線優等列車沿革]]を参照。
== 駅名 == *[[宮島駅]] - [[JR西日本宮島フェリー]][[宮島航路]]の駅 *[[宮島駅_(長野県)]] - [[上田温泉電気軌道青木線]]の駅（[[1938年]]廃止） *宮島駅 - 現在の[[宮島口駅]]の、[[1942年]]までの名称
== その他 == * 宮島 - [[しゃもじ]]の異称。
{{aimai}}

== 島 == *[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]内の[[島]]、「[[厳島]]」の一般的な呼び名。安芸の宮島。詳細は[[厳島]]を参照のこと。
== 地名 == * [[宮島 (今金町)]] - [[北海道]][[瀬棚郡]][[今金町]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (阿賀野市)]] - [[新潟県]][[阿賀野市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (上越市)]] - 新潟県[[上越市]][[板倉区]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (富士市)]] - [[静岡県]][[富士市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (茨木市)]] - [[大阪府]][[茨木市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名。 * [[宮島町]] - 上記「厳島」に存在した自治体。[[2005年]]に廿日市市へ編入。
== 姓、名字 == *[[宮島竜治]] - 編集技師、[[映画監督]] *[[宮島喬]] - [[社会学者]] *[[宮島里奈]] - [[お笑い芸人]]

revision.comment: 浦富海岸の宮島を追加。
revision.text:
'''宮島'''（みやじま、みやしま） 古くは「宮嶋」とも表記し、[[名字]]としてはこちらを用いることもある。
== 島 == * [[広島県]][[廿日市市]]内の[[島]]、「[[厳島]]」の一般的な呼び名。安芸の宮島。 * [[宮島 (秋田県)]] - [[秋田県]][[男鹿市]]にある島。 * 宮島 (鳥取県) - [[鳥取県]][[岩美町]]にある。かつては島だったが、今は地続きになっている。島のうえにある荒砂神社は[[式内社]]の「大神社」に比定されている。付近にある「向島」と混同され、地図上では向島に「宮島」と書かれたものもある。[[鳥取県の島の一覧#宮島と向島]]参照。 * [[宮島 (熊本県)]] - [[熊本県]][[天草市]]にある島。
== 地名 == * [[宮島 (今金町)]] - [[北海道]][[瀬棚郡]][[今金町]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (阿賀野市)]] - [[新潟県]][[阿賀野市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (上越市)]] - 新潟県[[上越市]][[板倉区]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (富士市)]] - [[静岡県]][[富士市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (茨木市)]] - [[大阪府]][[茨木市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名。 * [[宮島町]] - 上記「厳島」に存在した自治体。[[2005年]]に廿日市市へ編入。
== 姓、名字 == * [[宮島竜治]] - 編集技師、[[映画監督]]。 * [[宮島喬]] - [[社会学者]]。 * [[宮島里奈]] - [[お笑い芸人]]。
== 列車 == * 急行「宮島」 - 旧[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]の[[急行列車]]（[[1972年]]廃止）。[[山陽本線優等列車沿革]]を参照。
== 駅名 == * [[宮島駅]] - [[JR西日本宮島フェリー]][[宮島連絡船]]の駅。 * [[宮島駅_(長野県)]] - [[上田温泉電軌青木線]]の駅（[[1938年]]廃止）。 * 宮島駅 - 現在の[[宮島口駅]]の、[[1942年]]までの名称。
== その他 == * [[しゃもじ]]の異称。
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやしま}} [[Category:同名の地名]] [[Category:日本語の姓]]

'''宮島'''（みやじま、みやしま） 古くは「宮嶋」とも表記し、[[名字]]としてはこちらを用いることもある。

== 列車 == * 急行「宮島」 - 旧[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]の[[急行列車]]（[[1972年]]廃止）。[[山陽本線優等列車沿革]]を参照。
== 駅名 == * [[宮島駅]] - [[JR西日本宮島フェリー]][[宮島連絡船]]の駅。 * [[宮島駅_(長野県)]] - [[上田温泉電軌青木線]]の駅（[[1938年]]廃止）。 * 宮島駅 - 現在の[[宮島口駅]]の、[[1942年]]までの名称。
== その他 == * [[しゃもじ]]の異称。

'''宮島'''（みやじま、みやしま） 古くは「宮嶋」とも表記し、[[名字]]としてはこちらを用いることもある。
== 島 == * [[広島県]][[廿日市市]]内の[[島]]、「[[厳島]]」の一般的な呼び名。安芸の宮島。 * [[宮島 (秋田県)]] - [[秋田県]][[男鹿市]]にある島。 * 宮島 (鳥取県) - [[鳥取県]][[岩美郡]][[岩美町]]にある。かつては島だったが、今は地続きになっている。島のうえにある荒砂神社は[[式内社]]の「大神社」に比定されている。付近にある「向島」と混同され、地図上では向島に「宮島」と書かれたものもある。[[鳥取県の島の一覧#宮島と向島]]参照。 * [[宮島 (熊本県)]] - [[熊本県]][[天草市]]にある島。
== 地名 == * [[宮島 (今金町)]] - [[北海道]][[瀬棚郡]][[今金町]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (阿賀野市)]] - [[新潟県]][[阿賀野市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (上越市)]] - 新潟県[[上越市]][[板倉区]]の地名。 * [[宮島街道]] - 広島県、西広島〜廿日市市間の[[国道2号]]の在来線。 * [[宮島 (富士市)]] - [[静岡県]][[富士市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (茨木市)]] - [[大阪府]][[茨木市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名。 * [[川島町宮島]] - [[徳島県]][[吉野川市]]の地名。 * [[宮島町]] - 上記「厳島」に存在した自治体。[[2005年]]に廿日市市へ編入。
== 姓、名字 == * [[宮島竜治]] - 編集技師、[[映画監督]]。 * [[宮島喬]] - [[社会学者]]。 * [[宮島里奈]] - [[お笑い芸人]]。
== 列車 == * 急行「宮島」 - 旧[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]の[[急行列車]]（[[1972年]]廃止）。[[山陽本線優等列車沿革]]を参照。
== 駅名 == * [[宮島桟橋]] - [[JR西日本宮島フェリー]][[宮島連絡船]]の駅。 * [[宮島駅 (長野県)]] - [[上田温泉電軌青木線]]の駅（[[1938年]]廃止）。 * 宮島駅 - 現在の[[宮島口駅]]の[[1942年]]までの名称。および、現在の[[広電宮島口駅]]の[[2001年]]までの名称。
== その他 == * [[しゃもじ]]の異称。安芸の宮島の名産品であることから。
{{aimai}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みやしま}} [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:同名の地名]] [[Category:日本語の姓]]

revision.comment: Created page with "== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊). == Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within..."
revision.text:
== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{Shinto-Buddhism}} {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub}}

{{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.

Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.

Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.

revision.text:
{{ill|Miyajima|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮島]], located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:廿日市市]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:広島県]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊藤博文]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大日如来]] as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:本地仏]] (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:虚空蔵菩薩]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神仏分離令]] (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:御山神社]] to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:狗賓]]
{{Buddhism2]] {{Shinto-Buddhism]] {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub]]
[[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Syncretic deities]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tengu]] [[Category:Oni]] [[ja:三鬼大権現]] [[Category:Sanki Daigongen forks]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Miyajima]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated open]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{ill|Miyajima|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:宮島]], located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:廿日市市]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:広島県]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:伊藤博文]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大日如来]] as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:本地仏]] (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:虚空蔵菩薩]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:神仏分離令]] (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:御山神社]] to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:New pages]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:狗賓]]

{{ill|Miyajima|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:宮島]], located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:廿日市市]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:広島県]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:伊藤博文]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大日如来]] as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:本地仏]] (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:虚空蔵菩薩]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:神仏分離令]] (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:御山神社]] to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:狗賓]]

{{ill|Miyajima|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:宮島]], located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:廿日市市]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:広島県]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:伊藤博文]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大日如来]] as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:本地仏]] (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:虚空蔵菩薩]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:神仏分離令]] (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:御山神社]] to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:狗賓]]

The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大日如来]] as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:本地仏]] (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:虚空蔵菩薩]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:弥山本堂]]. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:不動明王]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:神仏分離令]] (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:御山神社]] to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:狗賓]]

=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:空海]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:和讃]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:仏の一覧]] * {{ill|Gongen|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:権現]] * {{ill|Daitengu|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:大天狗]] * {{ill|Mountain worship|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:山岳信仰]] * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:三瀧寺]] * {{ill|Guhin|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyajima]][[Category:qq]] <!--Miyajima--> [[ja:狗賓]]

revision.text:
{{ill|Miyajima|en}} <!--Miyajima--> , located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en}} <!--Miyajima--> , {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en}} <!--Miyajima--> . Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en}} <!--Miyajima--> , Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}} <!--Miyajima--> as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en}} <!--Miyajima--> (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}} <!--Miyajima--> as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en}} <!--Miyajima--> . * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}} <!--Miyajima--> as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en}} <!--Miyajima--> (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}} <!--Miyajima--> to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en}} <!--Miyajima--> (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}} <!--Miyajima--> (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Gongen|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Daitengu|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Mountain worship|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Guhin|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
{{Buddhism2]] {{Shinto-Buddhism]] {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub]]

Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima--> , Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|en}} <!--Miyajima--> as the {{ill|honji-butsu|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima--> (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|en}} <!--Miyajima--> as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima--> . * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|en}} <!--Miyajima--> as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima--> (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|en}} <!--Miyajima--> to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima--> (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|en}} <!--Miyajima--> (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Gongen|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Daitengu|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Mountain worship|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en|12=draft}} <!--Miyajima-->
* {{ill|Guhin|en}} <!--Miyajima-->
{{Buddhism2]] {{Shinto-Buddhism]] {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub]]

36 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宮島 up to rev 98397689
revision.text:
'''宮島'''（みやじま、みやしま） 古くは「宮嶋」とも表記し、[[名字]]としてはこちらを用いることもある。
== 島 == * [[広島県]][[廿日市市]]内の[[島]]、「[[厳島]]」の一般的な呼び名。安芸の宮島。 * [[宮島 (秋田県)]] - [[秋田県]][[男鹿市]]にある島。 * 宮島 (鳥取県) - [[鳥取県]][[岩美郡]][[岩美町]]にある。かつては島だったが、今は地続きになっている。島のうえにある荒砂神社は[[式内社]]の「大神社」に比定されている。付近にある「向島」と混同され、地図上では向島に「宮島」と書かれたものもある。[[鳥取県の島の一覧#宮島と向島]]参照。 * 宮島 (長崎県) - [[長崎県]][[平戸市]]にある無人島「沖ノ島」の別名。 * [[宮島 (熊本県)]] - [[熊本県]][[天草市]]にある島。
== 地名 == * [[宮島 (今金町)]] - [[北海道]][[瀬棚郡]][[今金町]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (阿賀野市)]] - [[新潟県]][[阿賀野市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (上越市)]] - 新潟県[[上越市]][[板倉区]]の地名。 * [[宮島街道]] - 広島県、西広島〜廿日市市間の[[国道2号]]の在来線。 * [[宮島 (富士市)]] - [[静岡県]][[富士市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (茨木市)]] - [[大阪府]][[茨木市]]の地名。 * [[宮島 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名。 * [[川島町宮島]] - [[徳島県]][[吉野川市]]の地名。 * [[宮島町]] - 上記「厳島」に存在した自治体。[[2005年]]に廿日市市へ編入。
== 姓、名字 == * [[宮島竜治]] - 編集技師、[[映画監督]]。 * [[宮島喬]] - [[社会学者]]。 * [[宮島里奈]] - [[お笑い芸人]]。 * [[宮島恵子]] - 元[[バレーボール]]選手。
== 列車 == * 急行「宮島」 - 旧[[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]の[[急行列車]]（[[1972年]]廃止）。[[山陽本線優等列車沿革]]を参照。
== 駅名 == * [[宮島桟橋]] - [[JR西日本宮島フェリー]][[宮島連絡船]]の駅。 * [[宮島駅 (長野県)]] - [[上田温泉電軌青木線]]の駅（[[1938年]]廃止）。 * 宮島駅 - 現在の[[宮島口駅]]の[[1942年]]までの名称。および、現在の[[広電宮島口駅]]の[[2001年]]までの名称。
== その他 == * [[しゃもじ]]の異称。安芸の宮島の名産品であることから。
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやしま}} [[Category:同名の島]] [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:同名の地名]] [[Category:日本語の姓]] [[Category:人名の曖昧さ回避]]
